agentic_research.note: Voice responses must stay grounded in editable knowledge and recommend handoff when retrieval is thin or outside scope.

business_name.description: Name of the customer business the voice co-worker represents.

escalation_policy.description: When and how the co-worker should recommend human escalation.
escalation_policy.example: Escalate allergies, food-safety concerns, refunds, complaints, payment-card questions, missing orders, angry callers, and anything not grounded in the knowledge base.

input_folder.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

knowledge_text.description: Editable customer-service knowledge used as the initial RAG text for this run.
knowledge_text.example: Menu, prices, hours, pickup/delivery steps, and escalation rules for Otter Slice Pizza.

opening_message.description: First spoken message after the caller connects.
opening_message.example: Thanks for calling Otter Slice Pizza. What delicious trouble can I help you get into today?

output_folder.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

service_scope.description: Plain-language scope of what the co-worker should help customers with.
service_scope.example: Take pizza orders, explain menu options, collect pickup or delivery details, and summarize the order.

spark_host.description: SSH destination for the NVIDIA Spark box.

voice.description: TTS voice name or preset used by the Magpie service.

voice_https_port.description: HTTPS port for the Spark-hosted WebRTC voice page.

voice_local_proxy_port.description: Localhost HTTPS port that proxies browser traffic to Spark.

contracts.summary: A voice customer-service co-worker for a small business demo. Give it the business name, service scope, opening message, escalation rules, editable knowledge text, and optional sample/input folder; it starts a local WebRTC voice experience and writes service status, conversation logs, knowledge snapshots, and handoff-ready run artifacts to the output folder.
users: Restaurant operators, pizza shops, customer operations leads, small businesses, and AI operations teams.

intent.description: A voice customer-service co-worker for a small business demo. Give it the business name, service scope, opening message, escalation rules, editable knowledge text, and optional sample/input folder; it starts a local WebRTC voice experience and writes service status, conversation logs, knowledge snapshots, and handoff-ready run artifacts to the output folder.
intent.problem: Pizza shops need a friendly voice co-worker that can help callers place basic orders from editable menu knowledge while handing sensitive or uncertain calls to people.

safety.reason: The co-worker can converse with customers and recommend escalation, but it does not approve refunds, account changes, or other business actions by itself.

intent.target_users: Support teams, customer operations leads, small businesses, service desks, and AI operations teams.

knowledge_rag.grounding_policy: Use retrieved local blueprint knowledge to improve actor review prompts; do not invent facts when retrieval is unavailable.
knowledge_rag.privacy: index local blueprint knowledge only; do not index private run documents unless a future runtime explicitly opts in
knowledge_rag.purpose: Retrieve editable run knowledge and menu/service-policy snippets for grounded customer-service voice responses.

metadata.agent_role: Pizza-ordering voice co-worker that answers from editable menu knowledge, helps collect an order, asks clarifying questions, and recommends human handoff when it is unsure or outside scope.

agentic_research.note: Voice responses must stay grounded in editable knowledge and recommend handoff when retrieval is thin or outside scope.

metadata.benefit: Lets an operator launch a local OtterDesk run, update plain-text pizza menu knowledge, and hand callers a localhost HTTPS voice link backed by the Spark NVIDIA stack.
metadata.customizable_for: Pizza shops, takeout counters, small restaurants, delivery intake, and friendly food-ordering demos.
metadata.description: A voice customer-service co-worker for a small business demo. Give it the business name, service scope, opening message, escalation rules, editable knowledge text, and optional sample/input folder; it starts a local WebRTC voice experience and writes service status, conversation logs, knowledge snapshots, and handoff-ready run artifacts to the output folder.

human_control.reason: The co-worker can converse with customers and recommend escalation, but it does not approve refunds, account changes, or other business actions by itself.

fields.description: The name the voice co-worker should use with callers.
fields.label: Business name

fields.default: Take pizza orders from editable menu knowledge and recommend human handoff when unsure.

fields.label: Service scope

fields.description: Plain text menu and ordering knowledge seeded into the run and editable from the voice page.
fields.label: Knowledge text

fields.default: Escalate allergies, food-safety concerns, refunds, complaints, payment-card questions, missing orders, angry callers, and anything not grounded in knowledge.
fields.description: Rules for asking a human to take over.
fields.label: Escalation policy

fields.description: HTTPS/WebRTC voice page port on Spark.
fields.label: Spark HTTPS port

fields.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.
fields.label: Input folder

fields.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.
fields.label: Output folder

init_config_review.instruction: When running in OtterDesk, show these overwriteable fields during init config and ask the user to confirm or edit them before launch.

agentic_research.description: Bounded LLM-selected or actor-guided tool contract for domain work.

artifacts.description: Stable run-store files and domain-specific outputs.

human.description: Human notices, approval requests, responses, timeouts, and applied decisions.

input_skills.description: Connector-backed or reusable skill-backed input sources.

inputs.description: Resolved runtime inputs and adapter provenance.

knowledge_rag.description: Optional local blueprint knowledge retrieval used to ground actor prompts and tool planning.

logs.description: Runtime stdout/stderr and explicit log artifacts.

output_skills.description: Optional fan-out destinations after local run-store writes.

outputs.description: Primary result and final user-facing artifact.

resources.description: Resource samples such as token, runtime, connector, and disk usage.

streams.description: Named data or event streams exposed by the blueprint.

knowledge_rag.grounding_policy: Use retrieved local blueprint knowledge to improve actor review prompts; do not invent facts when retrieval is unavailable.
knowledge_rag.privacy: index local blueprint knowledge only; do not index private run documents unless a future runtime explicitly opts in
knowledge_rag.purpose: Retrieve editable run knowledge and menu/service-policy snippets for grounded customer-service voice responses.

metadata.llm_agent_role: Customer-service voice co-worker that answers from the customer knowledge base, asks clarifying questions when needed, and recommends escalation when it is unsure or outside scope.
metadata.name: Pizza Order Voice AI Co-worker
metadata.output: Visual detection observations with count, label, category, color, position, activity, confidence, and alert status.

final_artifact.description: The durable user-facing artifact should summarize the result, recommendation, confidence, evidence, next steps, and source references.

output_skills.activation: Set MN_BLUEPRINT_EVENTS_WS_URL and enable this output skill via config override to stream events.
output_skills.name: websocket_events

output_contract.stdout: single JSON object, event envelope, or bundle path

metadata.problem_solved: Pizza shops need a friendly voice co-worker that can help callers place basic orders from editable menu knowledge while handing sensitive or uncertain calls to people.

quick_test.description: Quick test mode uses the bundled pizza menu knowledge and skips external customer-specific inputs.

runtime_features: actor-style LLM specialists use retrieved policy context when available
metadata.simulation_type: Spark-hosted real-time WebRTC pizza-ordering conversation exposed through localhost with editable text RAG.

standard.execution_model: load_metadata -> resolve_config -> load_inputs -> start_run_store -> observe_simulation_state -> call_llm_agent -> evaluate_workflow_state -> route_by_context -> request_human_input -> apply_decision_to_simulation -> emit_events -> write_final_artifact -> fan_out_output_skills

metadata.target_user: Video operators, security reviewers, safety operations teams, and site-access coordinators.
metadata.target_users: Support teams, customer operations leads, small businesses, service desks, and AI operations teams.

video_source.description: Default mapped media endpoint consumed by the runtime worker. The host-side pre-launch hook prepares local media and service handles before launch.

web_ui.description: Local shared OtterDesk dashboard with localhost voice URL, service health, events, menu knowledge metadata, and conversation artifacts.

workers.role: Prepare Spark voice runtime and knowledge store
Host WebRTC voice conversation service on Spark

steps.goal: Validate Spark, prepare run knowledge, and make the NVIDIA voice stack ready.
steps.label: Prepare Voice Service

steps.goal: Run the localhost-proxied WebRTC page so a caller can place a pizza order with the co-worker.
steps.label: Serve Customer Conversation
